foods.name: Empty Bottle
foods.description: There's nothing in this bottle.

foods.name: Soybean Sprout
foods.description: A soybean seed that has sprouted and is ready to be planted.

foods.name: Wolfsbane
foods.description: A purple flower with a woody, unpleasant odor. Even the scent can make one ill, and every part of the plant is toxic. It is used in Alchemy to create poisons that are especially effective against wolves and lycanthropes.

foods.name: Wolfsbane Bouquet
foods.description:
A collection of unpleasant-smelling flowers. These will make you very sick.

Lasts 4 hours

You can only have one flower arrangement "benefit" at a time.

foods.name: Second-Wind Slushie
foods.description: Recovers 75 health and power, and recovers a bit more for 30 seconds

foods.name: Ultimate Energy Drink
foods.description: Recovers 100 power instantly, and recovers 120 more over 60 seconds

foods.name: Milk Cap Mushroom
foods.description: A delicious mushroom.

foods.name: Blood Mushroom
foods.description: A theoretically delicious mushroom.

foods.name: Fly Amanita
foods.description: A mildly poisonous toadstool which can be eaten after boiling.

foods.name: Plate
foods.description: An old brown plate, the kind vendors really like for some reason. Maybe there's a collectibles market you're unaware of.

foods.name: Heart
foods.description: A large mammal heart.

foods.name: Brain
foods.description: A brain.

foods.name: Milk Cap Mushroom Powder
foods.description: A pile of powdered Milk Cap mushrooms.

foods.name: Blood Mushroom Powder
foods.description: A pile of powdered Blood mushrooms.

foods.name: Iocaine Powder
foods.description: A pile of powdered Iocaine mushrooms. This is a deadly toxin to almost all life forms.

foods.name: Iocaine Practice Dose
foods.description: A carefully tweaked dose of iocaine powder, designed to make you very ill but not kill you, allowing the body to build up resistance to the deadly toxin.

foods.name: Fly Amanita Flakes
foods.description: A pile of fly amanita flakes. These are only the normal level of fatal when eaten (which is still 100%, but lower than some other mushrooms).

foods.name: Brass Nail
foods.description: A very large, well-made brass nail. Useful in Carpentry.

foods.name: Ultimate Mushroom Turret
foods.description:
A plantable organic turret, designed to be planted as a weapon by a seasoned Mycologist.

(Use the 'Mushroom Turret 10' ability to plant this turret.)

foods.name: Perfectly Round Pebble
foods.description: This is an especially round pebble. But it's still just a pebble, with probably very few uses.

foods.name: Mushroom Substrate (Dirt)
foods.description: Fertilizer for a mushroom box farm.

foods.name: Mushroom Substrate (Meat)
foods.description: Fertilizer for a mushroom box farm.

foods.name: Mushroom Substrate (Organs)
foods.description: Fertilizer for a mushroom box farm.

foods.name: Mushroom Substrate (Limbs)
foods.description: Fertilizer for a mushroom box farm.

foods.name: Mushroom Substrate (Exotic)
foods.description: Fertilizer for a mushroom box farm.

foods.name: Brain Bug Lobe
foods.description: A small lobe from a Brain Bug. Wait, did... did it just pulse? Did you see that? No... probably your imagination.

foods.name: Brass Gear
foods.description: A brass gear the size of your hand. It has no obvious purpose.

foods.name: Brass Figurine
foods.description: A small statue of a goblin. It was probably originally part of a child's toy army. Looks antique.

foods.name: Mushroom Box
foods.description: When placed in a damp cave, this box can be used to grow a crop of mushrooms.

foods.name: Fast Mushroom Box
foods.description:
This box can be used to grow a crop of mushrooms.
Grow Time: 20% faster than normal
XP Penalty: -50% XP per mushroom

foods.name: Very Fast Mushroom Box
foods.description:
This box can be used to grow a crop of mushrooms.
Grow Time: 40% faster than normal
XP Penalty: -50% XP per mushroom

foods.name: Mega-Fast Mushroom Box
foods.description:
This box can be used to grow a crop of mushrooms.
Grow Time: 60% faster than normal
XP Penalty: -50% XP per mushroom

foods.description:
This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.
Box Duration: 12 hours
Grow Time: 25% slower per crop
XP: +33% more per mushroom

foods.description:

XP: +33% more per mushroom

XP: +33% more per mushroom

foods.name: Shiny Plate
foods.description: Nice goblin craftsmanship. Well, goblin oversight of slave craftsmanship, which is the same thing.

foods.name: Lucky Mushroom Box
foods.description:
This box can be used to grow a crop of mushrooms.
Chance for Second Mushroom Type: 33%

foods.name: Lucky Mushroom Box (12 hour)
foods.description:
This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.
Box Duration: 12 hours
Chance for Second Mushroom Type: 33% per crop

foods.description:
This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.
Box Duration: 24 hours

foods.description:
This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.

foods.description:
This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.

foods.description:
This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.
Box Duration: 24 hours
Grow Time: 50% slower for each crop
Yield: +66% mushrooms per crop

foods.description:
This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.

Yield: +66% mushrooms per crop

This box can be used to grow mushrooms.
Box Duration: 24 hours

This box can be used to grow mushrooms.
Box Duration: 48 hours

Yield: +66% per crop

foods.description:
This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.
Box Duration: 48 hours
Grow Time: 50% slower for each crop
Yield: +100% mushrooms per crop

This box can grow multiple crops of mushrooms.
Box Duration: 72 hours
Grow Time: 50% slower for each crop
Yield: +100% mushrooms per crop

foods.name: Epic Crop Mushroom Box
foods.description:
When placed in a damp cave, this box can be used to grow a large crop of mushrooms.
Grow Time: 100% slower than normal
Yield: +200% more mushrooms than normal
Note: this box requires 2 doses of substrate

foods.name: Epic Crop Mushroom Box v2
foods.description:
When placed in a damp cave, this box can be used to grow a large crop of mushrooms.
Grow Time: 100% slower than normal
Yield: +220% more mushrooms than normal
Chance for Second Mushroom Type: 20%
Note: this box requires 2 doses of substrate

foods.name: Council Certificate
foods.description: Stamped with the seal of an official Council representative, this certificate is redeemable for 1000 councils at the Statehelm Bank.

foods.name: Anchor Rune
foods.description: A small runestone used by summoners to ensure the creatures they bring forth can't return to their native dimension. Clever animal handlers have found alternate uses for its potent magic.

foods.name: Cold Iron Shackles
foods.description: These shackles would only fit small creatures, such as fey or possibly dwarves.

foods.name: Red Game Chip
foods.description: A small painted wooden chip, used in card and parlor games.

foods.name: Green Game Chip
foods.description: A small painted wooden chip, used in card and parlor games. It has tiny flecks of inlaid gemstone, making it slightly harder for rogues to slip counterfeits into a game.

foods.name: Yellow Game Chip
foods.description: A small painted wooden chip, used in card and parlor games. The paint is magical, and protects it from being replicated by illusion spells -- a common ploy amongst dwarven rogues and hustlers.

foods.name: Heartshroom
foods.description: The fungal core of a myconian. It is inedible and has no obvious use, but it somehow feels useful. To somebody.

foods.name: Ancient Stencil
foods.description: A sort of "cheat sheet" for ancient scribes. Perhaps it'd be useful in recreating lost Calligraphy secrets.

foods.name: Demonic Skull
foods.description: This is the skull of some weird otherworldly creature. Neat.

foods.name: Orcish Spell Pouch
foods.description: A small leather sack of weird powders and bones, useful for casting Orcish magics. It is worn prominently by Orcish wizards as a symbol of their power.

foods.name: Ectoplasm
foods.description: Ghost sweat, congealed into a slimy lump. Probably useful for crafting potions or something.

foods.name: Powdered Mammal
foods.description: The desiccated remains of a small animal that was caught and partially digested by a carnivorous plant or giant spider or something horrible like that.

foods.name: Rubbery Tongue
foods.description: The long tongue of a frog-like creature.

foods.name: Fairy Wing
foods.description: The broken-off wing of a dead fairy. Broken fairy wings are a horrific, nauseating sight to other fairies.

foods.name: Troll Flesh
foods.description: A lump of flesh from a troll. It automatically regenerates at the same rate that it rots.

foods.name: Fetish Bag
foods.description: A small cloth satchel containing special powder that can be used to commune with the dead.

foods.name: Unidentified Painting
foods.description: This unidentified pastoral seems to be in good condition.

foods.name: Unidentified Painting
foods.description: This idyllic scene seems to have stood the passage of time quite well.

foods.name: Unidentified Painting
foods.description: This painting depicts a long-lost monument. Rather pretty.

foods.name: Unidentified Painting
foods.description: This nature scene was painted with vibrant colors.

foods.name: Unidentified Painting
foods.description: There is a hint of melancholy behind this beautiful nature scene.

foods.name: Unidentified Painting
foods.description: This pleasant abstract painting has a nice solid frame.

foods.name: Unidentified Painting
foods.description: A nature scene with an incredible level of detail.

foods.name: Brass Figurine of a Hag
foods.description: A small brass figurine of a hag. It was probably originally part of a child's toy army. Looks antique, and somewhat valuable.

foods.name: Minor Panacea
foods.description: Dispels most curses, diseases, disfigurements, addictions, and botherments.

foods.name: Bland Waterfall Painting
foods.description: This painting of a waterfall is bland and uninspired, but you can barely make out an older, better painting underneath. The older painting looks to have been a practice piece by a goblin expert.

foods.name: Numbered Ertrigard Painting
foods.description: This is an original numbered Ertrigard painting. The master is beloved for waterfall scenes like this one. However, thanks to his magic brush, he created over 40,000 paintings during his lifetime, so being "original" doesn't really make it too terribly special.

foods.name: Phot Painting
foods.description: This depiction of the Tower of Treason was made by an elven Phot craftsman, using their signature one-bristle brush.

foods.name: Painting of Ema
foods.description: A breathtaking scene of the old Council grounds of Ema. It seems to be 3000 years old. Sadly, however, there are traces of alteration magic in the paint, and it could well be a forgery.

foods.name: Peaceful Scene
foods.description: A lovely scene. Perhaps it was created by Mirkym -- it has her signature approach to foliage -- but probably just created in the same style by an admirer of her work.

foods.name: Pickled Brain
foods.description: A brain that's been preserved in some sort of alchemical soup.

foods.name: "Grim's Death"
foods.description: A recreation of the famous painting from the end of the Dwarven Period. The detail work really captures the ham-fisted spirit of dwarven painting techniques.

foods.name: Ornately-Framed Painting
foods.description: This quality replica of "Candlelight and Dawnlight" is overshadowed by its frame, a beautiful masterpiece without peer.

foods.name: "Pies Cooling On A Windowsill"
foods.description: This scribbled mess is supposedly about pies, but it doesn't contain any recognizable shapes -- just ugly blobs. It's unsigned and unappealing.

foods.name: Coinpurse
foods.description: A small sack of Councils.

foods.name: "Orcs and Storks"
foods.description: You can kind of make out little orc faces and little stork bodies in this abstract painting, although you have to squint. It's bright and upbeat, but you've never heard of the painter.

foods.name: "With The Wind On My Face I Can Never Smile"
foods.base_name: "With The Wind On My Face I Can Never Smile"
foods.description: A wintry woodland scene featuring a little elf girl crying. You don't recognize the artist's name, but it dates back at least 1300 years, so that's something.

foods.name: "Wheat"
foods.description: A well-known painting by the dwarven artist Kot Bhenul. Signed and numbered! This is a great find.

foods.name: Gargoyle Egg
foods.description: It's stone, egg-shaped, and it came off of a gargoyle. Whether it's an actual embryonic gargoyle is not readily apparent.

foods.name: Minor Panacea (Admin-Provided)
foods.description: Dispels most curses, diseases, disfigurements, addictions, and botherments.

foods.name: Lac
foods.description: Lac is secreted from some types of insects, most notably the lac insect. It is used in creating shellac, which is a versatile crafting resource.

foods.name: Extra-Gigantic Coin Sack
foods.description: A huge sack of Councils.

foods.name: Unidentified Painting
foods.description: This unidentified portrait is remarkably lifelike.

foods.name: Jack O' Lantern
foods.description: A classic scary carved pumpkin.

foods.name: Owl Jack O' Lantern
foods.description: A pumpkin carved with a silly owl face.

foods.name: Creepy Jack O' Lantern
foods.description: A pumpkin carved with a scary octopus face.

foods.name: Magic Teapot
foods.description: An enchanted teapot, useful for holding enchanted tea. (But this one is empty.)

foods.name: Small Firework
foods.description: A simple rocket that explodes in harmless light when used.

foods.name: Small Confetti Bomb
foods.description: A small ball of paper that explodes into a colorful burst when lit.

foods.name: Large Confetti Bomb
foods.description: A ball of paper that explodes into a colorful burst when lit.

foods.name: Purple Firework
foods.description: A simple rocket that explodes in harmless light when used.

foods.name: Purple Confetti Bomb
foods.description: A ball of paper that explodes into a colorful burst when lit.

foods.name: Green Firework
foods.description: A simple rocket that explodes in harmless light when used.

foods.name: Green Confetti Bomb
foods.description: A ball of paper that explodes into a colorful burst when lit.

foods.name: Confetti Barrel
foods.description: This barrel smells strongly of gunpowder. Light the fuse to release a swarm of confetti bomb rockets.

foods.name: Sparkler
foods.description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it sparkles for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.

foods.name: Red Sparkler
foods.description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it creates red sparkler for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.

foods.name: Green Sparkler
foods.description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it creates green sparkles for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.

foods.name: Blue Sparkler
foods.description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it creates blue sparkles for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.

foods.name: Sparkler Crate
foods.description:
A crate full of sparklers you can deploy for all to enjoy.

Can only be deployed once every two hours.

foods.name: Butter Muslin
foods.description: Cotton cloth that's extremely porous. Ideal for butter and certain cheeses.

foods.name: Fae Felt
foods.description: Impossibly soft felt cloth.

foods.name: Simple Blanket
foods.description: Thin and uncomfortable, but warm enough to keep you alive.

foods.name: Basic Saddle Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives extra padding to a saddle. (Can be used for warmth in an emergency.)

foods.name: Robust Saddle Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives extra padding to a saddle. (Can be used for warmth in an emergency.)

foods.name: Impressive Saddle Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives extra padding to a saddle. (Can be used for warmth in an emergency.)

foods.name: Retting Bundle
foods.description: A bundle of flax, ready to be water retted. Place in deep water. Takes 30 minutes to soak.

foods.name: Pastoral Mare Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with an Ice-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an emergency.)

foods.name: Rustic Mare Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Cold-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an

foods.name: Cozy Mare Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Cool-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an

foods.name: Sleek Mare Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Warm-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an

foods.name: Cooling Mare Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Hot-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an

foods.name: Svelte Mare Blanket
foods.description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Fire-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an

foods.name: Rabbit's Foot
foods.description: The bloody forelimb of a rabbit. It smells.

foods.name: Cockatrice Beak
foods.description: The beak of a cockatrice. Just touching it causes your hands to go numb momentarily. Creepy!

foods.name: Coin Sack
foods.description: A sack of Councils.

foods.name: Brass Screws
foods.description: So-called "Dwarven Nails", these aren't useful for making houses or other sensible things. Instead, they're used in creating weird machines.

foods.name: Brass Fishhook
foods.description: A large brass hook, used in advanced ice-fishing.

foods.name: Silver Spear Head
foods.description: The sharp part of a spear. It's made of silver, so perhaps it was intended to be used against lycanthropes or worm-demons.

foods.name: Silver Salad Fork
foods.description: Popular with silversmiths and salad enthusiasts.

foods.name: Coinpurse
foods.description: A sack of Councils.

foods.name: Big Coin Sack
foods.description: A big sack of Councils.

foods.name: Large Coin Sack
foods.description: A big sack of Councils.

foods.name: Huge Coin Sack
foods.description: A huge sack of Councils.

foods.name: Very Huge Coin Sack
foods.description: A huge sack of Councils.

foods.name: Brass Oddity
foods.description: This brass device serves no obvious purpose. Perhaps it was part of an ancient machine.

foods.name: Mummy Wrapping
foods.description: A piece of enchanted cloth from a mummy.

foods.name: Spider Egg
foods.description: Well... it hasn't hatched... yet... hopefully it won't!

foods.name: Power Syrup
foods.description: Very sweet, sludgy, vaguely plant-flavored.

foods.name: Dirty Snow Water
foods.description: Water made from freshly-melted snow, plus various impurities.

foods.name: Treant Acorn
foods.description: The seed of a treant.

foods.name: Golden Acorn
foods.description: At first glance it seems to be an acorn dipped in gold, but it's actually an acorn carved from solid gold. Pretty.

foods.name: Oak Mushroom Box Frame
foods.description: A framework for building a mushroom grow-box.

foods.name: Cedar Mushroom Box Frame
foods.description: A framework for building a mushroom grow-box.

foods.name: Spruce Mushroom Box Frame
foods.description: A framework for building a mushroom grow-box.

foods.name: Crimsonfelt Wings
foods.description: The wings of a dead crimsonfelt butterfly.

foods.name: Super-Shoddy Calligraph Bench
foods.description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x2.

foods.name: Shoddy Calligraphy Bench
foods.description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x3.

foods.name: Rough Calligraphy Bench
foods.description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x4.

foods.name: Awesome Calligraphy Bench
foods.description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x12.

foods.name: Fae Honeycomb
foods.description: A small piece of honeycomb from a fae beehive.

foods.name: Huge Fae Honeycomb
foods.description: A large chunk of honeycomb from a fae beehive.

foods.name: Super-Shoddy Chair
foods.description: A somewhat-comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +3 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Shoddy Chair
foods.description: A somewhat-comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +6 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Rough Chair
foods.description: A somewhat-comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +9 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Crude Chair
foods.description: A somewhat-comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +12 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Decent Chair
foods.description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +15 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Nice Chair
foods.description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +18 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Quality Chair
foods.description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +21 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Great Chair
foods.description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +24 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Amazing Chair
foods.description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +27 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Astounding Chair
foods.description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +30 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Awesome Chair
foods.description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +33 for 30 minutes.

foods.name: Phoenix Beak
foods.description: Gruesome!

foods.name: Empty Fletching Box
foods.description: Holds 1000 dowels. Suitable for use by fletchers. The box can only be used once.

foods.name: Phoenix Egg
foods.description: Quite warm, and quite bright: whatever's inside is alive!

foods.name: Fairy Iceheart
foods.description: Some extremely dedicated Winter Court fae replace their biological heart with one of these. Presumably it makes them more powerful...

foods.name: Emergency Warmth Fire
foods.description: This small fire is warm enough to keep you from freezing to death, but not good enough for much else. Lasts 60 seconds once deployed.

foods.name: Basic Campfire
foods.description: A fire pit, good for cooking in the field or for keeping warm in the cold. Lasts 3 minutes once deployed.

foods.name: Dronefire
foods.description: A robotic fire pit, which uses advanced automaton techniques to spread the warmth further and keep it going longer. Lasts 10 minutes once deployed.

foods.name: Overloaded Dronefire
foods.description:
A robotic fire pit, overloaded with a powerful source of energy. Lasts 10 minutes once deployed.

While standing near the deployed Dronefire, you receive the following:
+10% Cold Resistance
+10% Direct Fire Damage
+20% Fire Vulnerability

foods.name: Starfish
foods.description: A dead starfish.

foods.name: Shoddy Storage Crate
foods.description: A small box for storing up to 4 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)

foods.name: Crude Storage Crate
foods.description: A box for storing up to 8 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)

foods.name: Decent Storage Crate
foods.description: A box for storing up to 12 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)

foods.name: Nice Storage Crate
foods.description: A box for storing up to 16 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)

foods.name: Quality Storage Crate
foods.description: A box for storing up to 20 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)

foods.name: Great Storage Crate
foods.description: A box for storing up to 24 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)

foods.name: Amazing Storage Crate
foods.description: A box for storing up to 28 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)

foods.name: Astounding Storage Crate
foods.description: A box for storing up to 32 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)

foods.name: Kilderkin
foods.description: A mid-sized cask used for food and drink production.

foods.name: Simple Item Dispenser
foods.description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense free items. This model can dispense up to 10 of a single kind of item.

foods.name: Large-Capacity Item Dispenser
foods.description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense free items. This model can dispense up to 1000 of a single kind of item.

foods.name: Fancy Item Dispenser
foods.description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense free items. This model can dispense up to 1000 of a single kind of item and has a place for a custom message that all visitors will see.

foods.name: Simple Item Vendor
foods.description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense items and collect currency. This model can sell up to 10 of a single kind of item.

foods.name: Large-Capacity Item Vendor
foods.description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense items and collect currency. This model can sell up to 1000 of a single kind of item.

foods.name: Fancy Item Vendor
foods.description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense items and collect currency. This model can sell up to 1000 of a single kind of item and has a place for a custom message that all users will see.

foods.name: Demo-Player Item Vendor
foods.description: For Guide use only! This is a Fancy Item Vendor that only dispenses to Demo players.

foods.name: Simple Free Item Raffler
foods.description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense free raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 10 free raffle tickets for a single item (or item stack).

foods.name: Modest Free Item Raffler
foods.description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense free raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 20 free raffle tickets for 1 or 2 items (or item stacks) and/or a small cash prize.

foods.name: Standard Item Raffler
foods.description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 30 raffle tickets for up to 3 items (or item stacks) and/or a cash prize. The raffle tickets can be free or can cost Councils.

foods.name: Fancy Item Raffler
foods.description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 50 raffle tickets for up to 6 items (or item stacks) and/or a cash prize. The raffle tickets can be free or can cost Councils. This model has a place for an inscription that is displayed to each user.

foods.name: Advanced Item Raffler
foods.description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 100 raffle tickets for up to 8 items (or item stacks) and/or a cash prize. The raffle tickets can be free or can cost Councils. This model has a place for an inscription that is displayed to each user, and a message that is shown only to the winner. This model can also be configured to have a time limit of 1-120 minutes.

foods.name: Ultra Item Raffler
foods.description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 1000 raffle tickets for up to 10 items (or item stacks) and/or a cash prize. The raffle tickets can be free or can cost Councils. This model has a place for an inscription that is displayed to each user, and a message that is shown only to the winner. This model can also be configured to have a time limit of 1-120 minutes.

foods.name: Demo-Player Raffler
foods.description: For Guide use only! This is an Ultra Item Raffler that only allows Demo players to take tickets.

foods.name: Admin Item Raffler
foods.description: A raffler with all standard options, plus the ability to attune the items to the winner. For admin use.

foods.name: Paralyzing Rat Trap
foods.description: A simple spring-activated trap that will stun whatever walks over it. One-use.

foods.name: Cryogenic Pet Crate
foods.description: A magical freezing system for long-term pet storage. The pet is placed into suspended animation, unable to move, but still able to dream -- and to have nightmares, which cause the pet to lose all of its Happiness. This one-use container can hold any captive-bred (not wild-caught) Pet-Quality (non-breeding) animal for up to 90 days. Pets kept in this container for more than 90 days escape and are lost. [You can use this item on a stabled pet by visiting a stablemaster. Look for the "Use Item..." button on the stable screen.]

foods.name: Perfect Conch
foods.description: A lovely conch that has proven to be resilient and powerful. It's perfect for making magic weapons with!

foods.name: Miniature Egg Incubator
foods.description: A sack filled with strange substances that stay warm for a long time. (To use, give to a mother pet bee or wasp that is still guarding her larva in the 'gestation' stage. It will speed up incubation by 12 hours.)

foods.name: Super Egg Incubator
foods.description: A sack filled with strange substances that stay warm for a long time and smell reassuring to insects. (To use, give to a mother pet bee or wasp that is still guarding her larva in the 'gestation' stage. It will speed up incubation by 24 hours.)

foods.name: Articulated Cutting Arm
foods.description: A tool used aboard Fae ships during the repair of hull damage. It's quite heavy!

foods.name: Mortal-Realm Flora Sample
foods.description: It's a sealed pouch with something inside.

foods.name: Gigantic Coin Sack
foods.description: A huge sack of Councils.

foods.name: Gigantic Coin Sack
foods.description: A huge sack of Councils.

foods.name: Gigantic Coin Sack
foods.description: A huge sack of Councils.

foods.name: Gigantic Coin Sack
foods.description: A huge sack of Councils.

foods.name: Kuvou Tongue
foods.description: A slimy three-pronged tongue from a kuvou corpse.

foods.name: Frog
foods.description: A decent-sized frog. It's still alive, for now. It is comfortable being held and may have been someone's pet.

foods.name: Broken Fishing Net
foods.description: This large net has seen heavy use, and now needs significant repair... or, more likely, replacement.

foods.name: Iron Fishing Hook
foods.description: This high-quality fishing hook was made by an expert blacksmith.

foods.name: Povus Gur-Horta Leaves
foods.description: This strain of gur-horta is relatively weak, making it a poor choice for refining into combat potions. However, it's popular among orcs that smoke the leaves recreationally due to its mellow taste and low side-effects.

foods.name: Ice Bypass Spell
foods.description: It's a small parchment with a magical inscription. You can use this to get past a huge icy barrier beneath the ruined tower in Kur.

foods.name: Winter Fae Blood
foods.description: A drop of blood that's been affected by the fae realm. Winterprize crystals are known to be sensitive to this sort of blood. Carrying it in large quantities is unwise, however, as it can corrupt the bearer. (You cannot carry more than 6 at once.)

foods.name: Chalice of Life
foods.description: A bulky wooden cup full of greenish energy. You can sip this in your dying moments in order to come back to life. Only druids can consume this energy.

foods.name: Rahu Visitor's Permit
foods.description: A transferrable day-pass to visit the parts of Rahu that are usually off-limits to tourists. While this permit lasts, the citizens of Rahu will not attack you and shopkeepers will generally be willing to deal with you. The pass becomes invalidated if you attack any Rahu citizen, and the plateau where the Onkara train is still off-limits.

foods.name: Simple Arisetsu Blanket
foods.description: A thin blanket embroidered with a depiction of a beaming sun. This style of blanket is often given out by followers of Arisetsu, the god of warmth and hope.

foods.name: Unfertilized Dead Urak Egg
foods.description: A rotten urak egg. Completely valueless.

foods.name: "The Gods of Knowledge, Vol. 1"
foods.description: This series of books by the goblin sage Muligu details each god that might be able to help you with a knowledge-related question.

foods.name: "The Gods of Knowledge, Vol. 2"
foods.description: This series of books by the goblin sage Muligu details each god that might be able to help you with a knowledge-related question.

foods.name: "The Gods of Knowledge, Vol. 3"
foods.description: This series of books by the goblin sage Muligu details each god that might be able to help you with a knowledge-related question.

foods.name: "The Gods of Knowledge, Vol. 4"
foods.description: This series of books by the goblin sage Muligu details each god that might be able to help you with a knowledge-related question.

foods.name: Monsters and Mantids Free-Play Ticket
foods.description: This ticket entitles the bearer to a free game of Monsters and Mantids at any table in the Red Wing Casino.

foods.name: Lootmaster Free-Play Ticket
foods.description: This ticket entitles the bearer to a free game of Lootmaster Match-3 at the Red Wing Casino.

foods.name: Cashfall Free-Play Ticket
foods.description: This ticket entitles the bearer to a free game of Cashfall Match-3 at the Red Wing Casino.

foods.name: Sir Johnson Buck
foods.description: A piece of paper that says "This document is worth 1 (one) Sir Johnson Buck, redeemable at participating commissaries. Sir Johnson retains the right to confiscate this document if he thinks you stole it." Beneath the impeccably-handwritten note is an illegible scrawl that you assume is Sir Johnson's signature.

foods.name: The Sage's Lectern, Vol. 1
foods.description: The first volume in a series of travelogues and interviews by the semi-famous mage Reginald.

foods.name: The Sage's Lectern, Vol. 2
foods.description: The second volume in a series of travelogues and interviews by the semi-famous mage Reginald.

foods.name: The Origin of 'Tast's Pack'
foods.description: The title is dull, but the cover art suggests something... less dull. However, someone has torn out the front half of the book, leaving only appendices.

foods.name: Paper Boat
foods.description: This piece of parchment has been folded into the shape of a boat.

foods.name: Fairy Soul Anchor
foods.description: The strange substance in this bag can keep dying fairies from leaving this plane of existence. It costs 50 Fae Energy to use this item. It smells of honey. (This item is useless to non-fae.)

foods.name: Broken Augment
foods.description: A strange cube. It once held augmentation magic, but it's been damaged beyond repair. You might be able to salvage something from it... if you knew how to open it without shattering it.

foods.name: Caged Pet
foods.description: This crate contains one pet, ready for travel or just some R&R.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Serbule
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Eltibule
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Kur Mountains
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Ilmari Desert
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Sun Vale
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - New Prestonbule
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Fae Realm
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Serbule Hills
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Rahu
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Portal Summoning Device - Kur Tower
foods.description: A small handheld device studded with aquamarines that can summon a portal to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when all of the charges have been used up.

foods.name: Portal Summoning Device - Goblin Dungeon
foods.description: A small handheld device studded with aquamarines that can summon a portal to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when all of the charges have been used up.

foods.name: Portal Summoning Device - Ilmari Labyrinth
foods.description: A small handheld device studded with aquamarines that can summon a portal to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when all of the charges have been used up.

foods.name: Portal Summoning Device - Gazluk Keep
foods.description: A small handheld device studded with aquamarines that can summon a portal to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when all of the charges have been used up.

foods.name: Portal Summoning Device - The Wintertide
foods.description: A small handheld device studded with aquamarines that can summon a portal to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when all of the charges have been used up. IMPORTANT: Everyone must have a Transport Security Card in their inventory to use this portal!

foods.name: Creepy Starfish
foods.description: A dead starfish. It's covered in a strange goop that stings to the touch.

foods.name: Pennoc's Pension
foods.description: It's just a big sack of money.

foods.name: Liquid Soul Anchor
foods.description: A special potion that works similar to a Fairy Soul Anchor. Drinking this potion will grant a Fairy immunity from the Fae death penalty in the zone you are anchored to. IMPORTANT: You must drink this potion BEFORE you die! The effect will dispel if you leave the zone you are anchored to or logout.

foods.name: Foul Acorn
foods.description: An acorn found on an old treant that had been growing in mire for ages. It apparently couldn't grow in that soil, but perhaps there's similar soil elsewhere in Povus that would work?

foods.name: Broken Rake Handle
foods.description: From an Aktaari rake. They use them to cultivate evu fruit.

foods.name: Shoddy Music Box
foods.description: A poorly crafted music box that sounds like a lute for animal forms so they can play music for their plants. This box looks like it is only good for one use before it will break. Don't forget to start it up after you deploy it! IMPORTANT: You must be in beast form for at least 3 hours of in-game time before you can deploy this item. Leaving beast form after deploying will cause you to lose control of the box. Limit one music box deployment per account at a time.

foods.name: Sturdy Music Box
foods.description: A well crafted music box that sounds like a harp for animal forms so they can play music for their plants. This box looks sturdy enough for a few uses before it will break. Don't forget to start it up after you deploy it! IMPORTANT: You must be in beast form for at least 3 hours of in-game time before you can deploy this item. Leaving beast form after deploying will cause you to lose control of the box. Limit one music box deployment per account at a time.

foods.name: Glamour Berry
foods.description: A fae fruit that can make any piece of equipment look like an entirely different piece of equipment when worn. Just crush it up to absorb its power. [When you use this berry you'll receive one Glamour, and automatically learn the recipe to use Glamours if you don't already know how.]

foods.name: 10 LEC Voucher
foods.description: A voucher for 10 Live Event Credits. Credits will be given to the person that uses this voucher.

foods.name: 25 LEC Voucher
foods.description: A voucher for 25 Live Event Credits. Credits will be given to the person that uses this voucher.

foods.name: 50 LEC Voucher
foods.description: A voucher for 50 Live Event Credits. Credits will be given to the person that uses this voucher.

foods.name: 100 LEC Voucher
foods.description: A voucher for 100 Live Event Credits. Credits will be given to the person that uses this voucher.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Povus
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Glamour Gourd
foods.description: A fae fruit that can make any piece of equipment look like an entirely different piece of equipment when worn. Just smash it open to absorb its power. [When you use this gourd you'll receive one Glamour, and automatically learn the recipe to use Glamours if you don't already know how.]

foods.name: Portal Summoning Device - Fungal Fortress
foods.description: A small handheld device studded with aquamarines that can summon a portal to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when all of the charges have been used up. IMPORTANT: You must be friends with Mope in Povus to use this portal!

foods.name: Liquid Super Buffs! (24 Hours)
foods.description: Pop the top and chug it down for 24 hours of getting down! This foul tasting fizzy liquid will grant you super buffs for 24 hours of real time. This liquid will destablize and evaporate 60 days after it is created, so use it quickly! Buffs include: Combat & crafting xp. Extra fish, mushrooms, wood, seeds and fruit. Gardening bonus harvest chance. Survey and mining buffs. Training discount. Meal and snack time buffs. Vendor stall discount. NPC workorder bonus. Faster liver recovery. IMPORTANT!: There is no visible timer on these buffs, so be sure to note the time when you drink this!

foods.name: Liquid Super Buffs! (48 Hours)
foods.description: Pop the top and chug it down for 48 hours of getting down! This foul tasting fizzy liquid will grant you super buffs for 48 hours of real time. This liquid will destablize and evaporate 60 days after it is created, so use it quickly! Buffs include: Combat & crafting xp. Extra fish, mushrooms, wood, seeds and fruit. Gardening bonus harvest chance. Survey and mining buffs. Training discount. Meal and snack time buffs. Vendor stall discount. NPC workorder bonus. Faster liver recovery. IMPORTANT!: There is no visible timer on these buffs, so be sure to note the time when you drink this!

foods.name: Liquid Super Buffs! (24 Hours)
foods.description: Pop the top and chug it down for 24 hours of getting down! This foul tasting fizzy liquid will grant you super buffs for 24 hours of real time. This liquid will destablize and evaporate 60 days after it is created, so use it quickly! Buffs include: Combat & crafting xp. Extra fish, mushrooms, wood, seeds and fruit. Gardening bonus harvest chance. Survey and mining buffs. Training discount. Meal and snack time buffs. Vendor stall discount. NPC workorder bonus. Faster liver recovery. IMPORTANT!: There is no visible timer on these buffs, so be sure to note the time when you drink this!

foods.name: Giant Frog Food
foods.description: Made by ranalon to feed their pet frogs during low-insect times. They can also be eaten by ranalon in an emergency.

foods.name: Orange Hubris
foods.description: A pile of flaky rock. What could this be for?

foods.name: Writ of Unlock
foods.description: This writ is redeemable for 1 point during the Writ Challenge event.

foods.name: Golden Writ of Unlock
foods.description: This writ is redeemable for 25 points and 250,000 councils during the Writ Challenge event. Use this writ to get a special quest, then see the Writ Master located near the Serbule well to collect your prize.

foods.name: Portal Summoning Device - Nightmare Caves
foods.description: A small handheld device studded with aquamarines that can summon a portal to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when all of the charges have been used up.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Level 90 Trainers
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: The Traits of a Mount
foods.description: A thick treatise intended for those interested in breeding horses.

foods.name: Painclover
foods.description: A toxic weed of dubious utility.

foods.name: Keg of Love
foods.description: Use this item to deploy a Keg of Love which will explode and spread its love to everyone around it within 20 meters.

foods.name: Personal Teleportation Device - Vidaria
foods.description: A small handheld device with a large amethyst set in the center used to teleport the wielder to an attuned location. This device will self destruct when the charge has expired.

foods.name: Mnemosyne
foods.description: A weird container made of crystal. Inside is the essence of knowledge in liquid form. Used by the most advanced crafters.

foods.name: Loaded Die (Mystery)
foods.description: A six-sided die with a hidden gizmo that causes it to land on one side more often than others. You'll have to roll it to figure out what it's weighted for.

foods.name: Statehelm War Bond (1m)
foods.description: One year after issuance, the upstanding citizen bearing this document may redeem it at the Statehelm Bank.

foods.name: Statehelm War Bond (10m)
foods.description: One year after issuance, the upstanding citizen bearing this document may redeem it at the Statehelm Bank.

foods.name: Graffiti Cleaner
foods.description: Powdered soap. Used with a bucket of water to remove graffiti.

foods.name: Maggot
foods.description: It won't stop squirming!

foods.name: Diary of a Failed Maid
foods.description: "The sensational best-selling diary of a maid from the Crone Hegemony. This was saved from obscurity due to one scene that documents the first sabotage attack of the (at-the-time-unrecognized) Night Executioner, the deadly hitman who is STILL. AT. LARGE. Read of his first heinous exploits here!"

foods.name: Ranalon Beacon Activator
foods.description: Activate this gross beacon in a certain place to let ranalon know it's time to invade.

foods.name: Striped Pebble
foods.description: Beautiful! Look at those stripes!

foods.name: Acur Blood
foods.description: A dollop of blood from the realm known as "Acur", where blood flows as freely as water does here.

foods.name: Abrasive Sand
foods.description: This bulky sack contains especially hard sand. It is used in creating abrasives.

foods.name: Sandbag
foods.description: This bulky sack contains sand. It's clean, mid-grain sand.

foods.name: Lifeblood
foods.description: The last pulse of blood from a dying mortal taken by a vampire. These few drops of blood contain a sliver of the victim's soul.

foods.name: Cerberus Brainstem
foods.description: An elaborate network of nerves that connects the three heads together.

foods.name: Vidaria Gur-Horta Leaves
foods.description: This strain of gur-horta is relatively weak, making it a poor choice for refining into combat potions. However, it's popular among orcs that smoke the leaves recreationally due to its vibrant aroma and low side-effects.

foods.name: Accelerated Rotor
foods.description: A device that spins furiously when you hold it a certain way.

foods.name: Hematic Ichor
foods.description: A potent mixture of magical blood.

foods.name: Demonic Statement
foods.description: A factual statement about demons. Collect enough of these and Ferris Blueheart in Vidaria can turn them into a Scroll of Demonic Research.

foods.name: Chilling Goblet
foods.description: A very old goblet that remains cold to the touch even in hot weather. It's made of a strange metal that might be worth studying.

foods.name: Wind Heart
foods.description: A weird puff of animated air. It's odd how it doesn't disperse.

foods.name: Super-Gigantic Coin Sack
foods.description: A huge sack of Councils.

foods.name: Quartz Sand
foods.description: This is great for making bottles.

foods.name: "The Gods, Vol. XI: Arisetsu"
foods.description: A volume of the famous 37-book series describing the history of the gods. This edition includes annotations and commentary from the Council.

foods.name: VIP Token
foods.description: Use this item to claim 1 month of VIP membership.

foods.name: Memory Inhibitor
foods.description: Rub this potion into your hair and head and your memory will stop working so well.

foods.name: Rage Inhibitor
foods.description: Makes your attacks generate 25% less Rage, preventing enemies from using their Rage attacks as often.

foods.name: Rage Inhibitor Extreme
foods.description: Makes your attacks generate 50% less Rage, preventing enemies from using their Rage attacks as often.

foods.name: Perfect Memory Inhibitor
foods.description: Rub this potion into your hair and head and your short-term memory will break.

foods.name: Memory Enhancer
foods.description: A dollop of ooze in a tiny vial.

foods.name: Greater Memory Enhancer
foods.description: A tiny vial full of ooze of some sort.

foods.name: Ferocity Juice
foods.description: Boosts all of your attacks, but your maximum Power will be significantly reduced. Tastes like sludge.

foods.name: Sanguinizer
foods.description: A small vial of foul liquid. Its formula is a secret known only to werewolf clans.

foods.name: Ruggedizer
foods.description: Bits of meat are floating in this small vial of sugar water.

foods.name: Gur-Horta
foods.description: A popular orcish drug that overcharges your brainstem. That sounds horrifying, and it kind of is, but this low dose generally has few side-effects.

foods.name: Auk Gur-Horta
foods.description: A popular orcish drug that overcharges your brainstem. This advanced purified version of the drug is quite strong.

foods.name: Gruesome Spooky Punch
foods.description: A party bowl of disgusting substances. Anyone who drinks it becomes violently ill... with emphasis on the violent.

foods.name: Mercury
foods.description: A vial of the strange substance known as mercury or quicksilver. It is used in hatmaking, gadget creation, and alchemy. Under no circumstances should it be drunk.

foods.name: Cauldron of Vile Thoughts
foods.description:
The liquid in this cauldron is sure to cause hallucinations and may bring out latent magic effects in unpredictable ways. It's insanely dangerous.

Once deployed, any player can drink from the cauldron. It disappears after five minutes.

foods.name: Telka's Tail
foods.description: A pungent perfume that rakshasa find irresistable, but most other races find rather displeasant. (Orcs generally tolerate the smell well, and some even like it.) The perfume smells faintly of raw meat, cinnamon, and daisies.

foods.name: Darkness of the Pure
foods.description: A musky elvish perfume currently in vogue in Verta. Its complex scent can mask dirt and odors even from cleanliness-loving elves.

foods.name: Ravager
foods.description: A spicy perfume concocted by humans. A commercial failure at its goal of helping human men woo women, it found new life among solitary huntsmen, foresters, and others (of both sexes) who feel stronger and more bad-ass while wearing it.

foods.name: Dwarven Fixer
foods.description: This toxic compound burns the body, but it masks the symptoms of alcohol withdrawal. Carried in every dwarven medical kit.

foods.name: Holy Warmth
foods.description: Created by priests of Arisetsu, this pleasant drink wards away the cold.

foods.name: Fruitbreath Vial
foods.description: A vial of Fruitbreath Juice.

foods.name: Grizlark
foods.description: A jar of deadly acid with a grassy smell. It supposedly has many uses ranging from cleaning armor to cooking meals.

foods.name: Cranium Powder
foods.description: Powdered brains of psychic creatures. A choice drug for psychics.

foods.name: Pure Cranium Powder
foods.description: Powdered brains of psychic creatures. A choice drug for psychics. This batch is 99% pure frontal cortex.

foods.name: Enhanced Cranium Powder
foods.description: Powdered brains of psychic creatures. A choice drug for psychics. This batch has been enhanced with secret chemicals.

foods.name: Vilified Cranium Powder
foods.description: Powdered brains of psychic creatures, cut with secret and dangerous substances that enhance its effect on psychics.

foods.name: Pineal Juice
foods.description: The enchanted and ground-up pineal glands of intelligent creatures, in a sweet syrupy liquid.

foods.name: Pure Pineal Juice
foods.description: The enchanted and ground-up pineal glands of intelligent creatures. No syrup added, just pure pineal juice.

foods.name: Enhanced Pineal Juice
foods.description: The enchanted and ground-up pineal glands of intelligent creatures, mixed with a secret catalyst for added effect.

foods.name: Faux Pineal Juice
foods.description: A lemony liquid designed to counter the effects of severe Pineal Juice abuse.

foods.name: Cranked Pineal Juice
foods.description: The enchanted and ground-up pineal glands of intelligent creatures, mixed with a secret catalyst for added effect.

foods.name: Bounceweed
foods.description: A packet of strange chemicals wrapped in a leaf. It's said that some sentient animals find this drug irresistible.

foods.name: Northern Bounceweed
foods.description: A packet of strange chemicals wrapped in a leaf. It's said that some sentient animals find this drug irresistible.

foods.name: Pure Bounceweed
foods.description: A packet of strange chemicals wrapped in a leaf. It's said that some sentient animals find this drug irresistible.

foods.name: Augmented Bounceweed
foods.description: A packet of strange chemicals wrapped in a leaf. It's said that some sentient animals find this drug irresistible.

foods.name: Calmweed
foods.description: A packet of strange chemicals wrapped in a leaf. While it has minor benefits, its primary use is to cure Bounceweed hangovers. It can also temporarily relieve the symptoms of Bounceweed abuse. Calmweed is not addictive.

foods.name: Sharpen
foods.description:
A thin tube full of liquid. Apparently you jab it into your skin to use it -- a horrific idea that only a mantis could dream up.

Sharpen is supposedly not addictive.

foods.name: Super Sharpen
foods.description:
A thin tube full of liquid. Apparently you jab it into your skin to use it -- a horrific idea that only a mantis could dream up.

Sharpen is supposedly not addictive, but higher-quality doses are only effective if the recipient has well-honed survival instincts.

foods.name: Ultra Sharpen
foods.description:
A thin tube full of liquid. Apparently you jab it into your skin to use it -- a horrific idea that only a mantis could dream up.

Sharpen is supposedly not addictive, but higher-quality doses are only effective if the recipient has well-honed survival instincts.

foods.name: Perfect Sharpen
foods.description:
A thin tube full of liquid. Apparently you jab it into your skin to use it -- a horrific idea that only a mantis could dream up.

Sharpen is supposedly not addictive, but higher-quality doses are only effective if the recipient has well-honed survival instincts.

foods.name: Telka's Teeth ("Molar" Variety)
foods.description: A small vial that reeks of urine, gore, and chemicals. Only rakshasa are able to stomach this substance, and even they find it revolting -- although many rakshasa claim the smell is also strangely exhilarating.

foods.name: Telka's Teeth ("Incisor" Variety)
foods.description: A small vial that reeks of urine, gore, and chemicals. Only rakshasa are able to stomach this substance, and even they find it revolting -- although many rakshasa claim the smell is also strangely exhilarating.

foods.name: Telka's Teeth ("Canine" Variety)
foods.description: A small vial that reeks of urine, gore, and chemicals. Only rakshasa are able to stomach this substance, and even they find it revolting -- although many rakshasa claim the smell is also strangely exhilarating.

foods.name: Basic Volatile Ink
foods.description: Ink infused with fiery chemicals. Used by sigil scripters to create deadly runes.

foods.name: Advanced Volatile Ink
foods.description: Ink infused with fiery chemicals. Used by sigil scripters to create deadly runes.

foods.name: Expert Volatile Ink
foods.description: Ink infused with fiery chemicals. Used by sigil scripters to create deadly runes.

foods.name: Basic Holistic Ink
foods.description: Ink infused with many chemicals. It has many uses, but is most prominently used by sigil scripters to create restorative runes.

foods.name: Advanced Holistic Ink
foods.description: Ink infused with many chemicals. It has many uses, but is most prominently used by sigil scripters to create restorative runes.

foods.name: Expert Holistic Ink
foods.description: Ink infused with many chemicals. It has many uses, but is most prominently used by sigil scripters to create restorative runes.

foods.description: Ink infused with deadly chemicals. It has several uses, but is most commonly used by sigil scripters to create destructive runes.

foods.description: Ink infused with deadly chemicals. It has several uses, but is most commonly used by sigil scripters to create destructive runes.

foods.description: Ink infused with fiery chemicals. Used by sigil scripters to create deadly runes.
